zh.lifeSummary: 當Hannah Powell於1641年8月21日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂時，她的父親Thomas Powell II25歲，母親Priscilla Whitson21歲。她於1661年5月21日與Thomas Tuttle在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1710年10月15日在她的家鄉享年69歲，埋葬在美國維吉尼亞州丹維爾葛洛夫 史崔特 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hannah Powell于1641年8月21日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂时，她的父亲Thomas Powell II25岁，母亲Priscilla Whitson21岁。她于1661年5月21日与Thomas Tuttle在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1710年10月15日在她的家乡享年69岁，埋葬在美国弗吉尼亚州丹維爾葛洛夫 史崔特 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Powell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Hywel ‘son of Hywel’, a personal name meaning ‘eminent’ (see Howell ).
Irish: mainly of Welsh origin as in 1 above, but sometimes a surname adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Mac Giolla Phóil ‘son of the servant of Saint Paul’ (see Guilfoyle ).
English: variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .

variants.name: Powel
meanings: North German: from a form of the personal name   Paul  .

variants.name: Howell
meanings: Welsh and English: in the Welsh border counties, from the Welsh male personal name Hywel ‘eminent’, popular since the Middle Ages particularly in honor of the great 10th-century law-giving Welsh king. The name may also arise from the Breton personal name Houuel, Huwel, Huwal, Howael, cognate with the Welsh name. Bretons were especially numerous in the eastern counties of England. See Powell .
English: perhaps from a Middle English personal name Huwel (spelled Howel), a shortening of Huwelot or Huelin (see Hewlett and Huling ), both pet forms of Middle English Huw (see Hugh ).
English: habitational name from Howell (Lincolnshire), from an uncertain initial element + Old English wella ‘well, spring, stream’.

variants.name: Powles
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of Powells, itself a variant of Powell , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Hannah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the mother of the prophet Samuel (1 Samuel 1:2), Hebrew Hanna. It is derived from a Hebrew word meaning ‘He (i.e. God) has favoured me (i.e. with a child)’. See also Anne . This form of the name was taken up as a given name by the Puritans in the 16th and 17th centuries and remained popular until the late 19th century. Thereafter it fell somewhat from favour but has enjoyed a massive revival since the 1990s.

variants.name: Hanna
meanings: Either the biblical name ( see Hannah ) or a short form of Johanna .

variants.name: Hana
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.description: The city served as co-capital of Connecticut from 1701 until 1873
The Connecticut Colony granted the town petition for Township in May 1707 and colonists changed the name from Iron Works Village to East Haven

en.lifeSummary: When Hannah Powell was born on 21 August 1641, in Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, her father, Thomas Powell II, was 25 and her mother, Priscilla Whitson, was 21. She married Thomas Tuttle on 21 May 1661, in Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 15 October 1710, in her hometown, at the age of 69, and was buried in Grove Street Cemetery, Danville, Virginia, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hannah Powell est née le 21 août 1641, à Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Thomas Powell II, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Priscilla Whitson, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Tuttle le 21 mai 1661, à Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 15 octobre 1710, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Grove Street Cemetery, Danville, Virginie, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hannah Powell am 21. August 1641 in Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Powell II 25 und ihre Mutter Priscilla Whitson 21. Sie hat am 21. Mai 1661 in Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America Thomas Tuttle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 15. Oktober 1710 im Alter von 69 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Grove Street Cemetery, Danville, Virginia, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hannah Powell è nata il il 21 agosto 1641 nel Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, sua padre, Thomas Powell II, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Priscilla Whitson, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Tuttle il 21 maggio 1661, nel Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 15 ottobre 1710, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolta a Grove Street Cemetery, Danville, Virginia, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Hannah Powellが1641年8月21日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンニューヘイベン トウンで生まれた時，父Thomas Powell IIは25歳，母Priscilla Whitsonは21歳であった。1661年5月21日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンニューヘイベン トウンでThomas Tuttleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1710年10月15日に故郷で69歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国バージニア州ダンヴィルグローブ ストレエト セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hannah Powell(이)가 1641년 8월 21일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 뉴하븐 타운에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Powell II(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Priscilla Whitson(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1661년 5월 21일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 뉴하븐 타운에서 Thomas Tuttle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1710년 10월 15일 그녀의 고향에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 버지니아 주 단아빌 그로브 스트릿 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hannah Powell nasceu em 21 de agosto de 1641, em Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America, seu pai, Thomas Powell II, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Priscilla Whitson, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Tuttle em 21 de maio de 1661, em Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 15 de outubro de 1710, em sua cidade natal, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em Grove Street Cemetery, Danville, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hannah Powell родилась 21 августа 1641 г. в местности нюхавен тоун, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени Thomas Powell II было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Priscilla Whitson, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Tuttle 21 мая 1661 г. в местности нюхавен тоун, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 15 октября 1710 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности гров стрит кеметири, данвилл, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hannah Powell nació el 21 de agosto de 1641, en Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, Thomas Powell II, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Priscilla Whitson, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Thomas Tuttle el 21 de mayo de 1661, en Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 15 de octubre de 1710, en su pueblo, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultada en Grove Street Cemetery, Danville, Virginia, Estados Unidos.
